summary: Cortez has a verified affordable-housing record anchored by Tawi Kaan, a 42-unit permanent supportive housing project on Empire Street, the 2023 Housing Needs Assessment and Housing Action Plan, a 2023 Proposition 123 opt-in baseline, the 2025 Land Use Code rewrite, the Park Village West 7th Street RFQ, and regional capacity through Region 9 and HomesFund.

paragraphs.text: Tawi Kaan Apartments at 560 E. Empire Street is Cortez’s first permanent supportive housing development. The three-story building has 42 units, split evenly among one-, two-, and three-bedroom homes, for households making 30% or less of area median income. The project celebrated its grand opening on October 15, 2024, and CHFA helped finance it with a roughly $1.2M tax credit award.

The Tawi Kaan partnership includes BlueLine Development, The Piñon Project Family Resource Center, Boston Financial, and the Ute Mountain Ute Tribe. In 2025, the Affordable Housing Tax Credit Coalition recognized Tawi Kaan with the Charles L. Edson Recognition of Housing Tax Credit Excellence in the Native American Housing category.

The City completed its Housing Needs Assessment in May 2023 and published a 2023 Housing Action Plan. The needs assessment found that from 2020 to 2022, housing costs increased by 15.7% per year, while the action plan used the assessment to set goals, objectives, and priority actions for community housing.

Cortez City Council unanimously adopted a new Land Use Code on March 11, 2025, replacing the roughly 30-year-old prior code. The article identifies housing flexibility as a code goal and notes that the Manufactured Home District in south Cortez was changed to Mixed Residential.

In 2023, the City of Cortez, Town of Dolores, Town of Mancos, Montezuma County, and the Ute Mountain Ute Tribe opted in to Proposition 123. The three-year affordable-stock growth commitments are Cortez 89 units, Mancos 14, Dolores 26, Montezuma County 28, and the Ute Mountain Ute Tribe 5.

On March 26, 2025, the City issued the Park Village Housing RFQ for housing on the eastern 7.01 acres of a 14.02-acre city-owned site on West 7th Street between South Oak Street and South Chestnut Street. Proposals were due May 6, 2025, and the City preferred workforce housing affordable to households between 80% and 120% AMI.

Region 9 Economic Development District serves Archuleta, Dolores, La Plata, Montezuma, and San Juan counties, the cities and towns in those regions, and the Southern Ute and Ute Mountain Ute Indian Tribes.

HomesFund offers mortgage and down-payment assistance in southwest Colorado, including Montezuma County. The Journal reported that a $3.545M Proposition 123 award to HomesFund would be shared among Montezuma, La Plata, Dolores, San Juan, and Archuleta counties and was projected to facilitate homeownership for 42 homes over two years.
